currentTask.name: Integrate prompting best practices as self-prompt contracts
currentTask.goal: Turn frontier prompting best practices into a compact internal self-check before routing, research, planning, and implementation

environment.note: This workspace runs on Debian/WSL2. Global OpenCode runtime config is at /home/namikaz/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Added prompt-contract.sh as a compact self-prompt checklist
Updated workflow-router.sh to report the ask/proceed policy from the prompt contract
Updated /research, /plan, and /implement commands to run prompt-contract before phase work
Updated AGENTS, README, hub quickstart, provider runtime docs, and workspace overview to document prompt contracts
Propagated prompt-contract.sh and command files to topic folders
verification: bash ./scripts/prompt-contract.sh "maybe build a thing for reality from fundamental rules" --phase research
bash ./scripts/prompt-contract.sh "implement the next verified slice" --phase implement
residualRisk: Prompt contracts are intentionally heuristic; they improve self-checking but still rely on the orchestrator to ask only genuinely high-impact questions.
immediateNextSteps: Use normal prompts and let /route plus /prompt-contract decide when to ask questions
If the model asks too many or too few questions, tune prompt-contract heuristics with examples
Later add a small routing/prompt-contract eval set
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
